text.title: DDD Domain Events Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns observed when wiring domain events. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Handler chain mutates `event.payload`; downstream consumers receive different payload than producer raised.
antipattern.root-cause: Event class uses regular `class` with public attributes instead of frozen record.
antipattern.fix: Convert to `@dataclass(frozen=True)` / `record`. Adjust handlers to compute new events for derived facts.

antipattern.symptom: Application service dispatches `OrderPlaced` before `session.commit()`. Transaction rolls back; downstream system has acted on a non-existent order.
antipattern.root-cause: Dispatcher called inside the aggregate command or directly after `session.flush()` but before commit.
antipattern.fix: Defer collection to after `session.commit()`. In SQLAlchemy use `after_commit` event hook; in EF Core call `_db.SaveChangesAsync(ct)` then `aggregate.CollectEvents()`.

antipattern.symptom: App writes to DB then publishes to Kafka. DB commits, Kafka publish fails (network blip) — event lost. Or Kafka publishes, DB rolls back — phantom event consumed.
antipattern.root-cause: Direct broker publish from the application service.
antipattern.fix: Insert event into `outbox` table in the same transaction as the state change. Separate relay (Debezium / poll loop) reads unprocessed rows and publishes to broker with at-least-once + dedup by `event_id`.

antipattern.symptom: Consumer re-processes the same event on broker redelivery. Side effects fire twice (duplicate email, duplicate charge).
antipattern.root-cause: Event has no `event_id`; consumer cannot dedup.
antipattern.fix: Add UUID `event_id` to event constructor; consumer stores processed IDs in a `processed_events` table (or Redis set) and skips duplicates.
